sourceMovie.title: It Follows
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto It Follows (2015) as a horror theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The horror and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Scream
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The horror energy of It Follows is alive in Scream (1996), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a dark payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Fear Street: 1994
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fear Street: 1994 (2021) operates in the same horror as It Follows, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The dark payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Malignant
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Malignant (2021) belongs to the same conversation as It Follows — both anchor their horror in mystery emotion that doesn't let go. The dark undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Fear Street: 1978
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If It Follows's horror stayed with you, Fear Street: 1978 (2021) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The mystery through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: What Lies Beneath
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where It Follows excels in horror, What Lies Beneath (2000) doubles down on mystery to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Dressed to Kill
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Dressed to Kill (1980) is the natural next step after It Follows: same horror DNA, different perspective. The dark streak and mystery elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: The Entity
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of It Follows who respond to its horror will find The Entity (1982) immediately familiar. Both films share a mystery sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Body Cam
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason It Follows fans keep landing on Body Cam (2020) — the horror is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Sherlock Holmes
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Sherlock Holmes draws from the same mystery well as It Follows, channeling adventure in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2009 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: The Conjuring 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Conjuring 2 (2016) captures the horror appeal of It Follows without simply imitating it. The dark streak running through both films is what puts The Conjuring 2 at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

faq.question: What should I watch after It Follows?
faq.answer: Try Scream, Fear Street: 1994, Malignant—each shares DNA with It Follows while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is It Follows?
faq.answer: It Follows (2015) is best described as Horror, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is It Follows part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "It Follows Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
